0	/**
1	 * @file Pipelines provide a high-level, easy to use, API for running machine learning models.
2	 *
3	 * **Example:** Instantiate pipeline using the `pipeline` function.
4	 * ```javascript
5	 * import { pipeline } from '@huggingface/transformers';
6	 *
7	 * const classifier = await pipeline('sentiment-analysis');
8	 * const output = await classifier('I love transformers!');
9	 * // [{'label': 'POSITIVE', 'score': 0.999817686}]
10	 * ```
11	 *
12	 * @module pipelines
13	 */
14	
15	import {
16	    AutoTokenizer,
17	    PreTrainedTokenizer,
18	} from './tokenizers.js';

20	    AutoModel,

34	    AutoModelForImageSegmentation,

36	    AutoModelForUniversalSegmentation,
37	    AutoModelForObjectDetection,
38	    AutoModelForZeroShotObjectDetection,
39	    AutoModelForDocumentQuestionAnswering,
40	    AutoModelForImageToImage,
41	    AutoModelForDepthEstimation,
42	    AutoModelForImageFeatureExtraction,
43	    PreTrainedModel,
44	} from './models.js';

46	    AutoProcessor,
47	} from './models/auto/processing_auto.js';
48	import {
49	    Processor,
50	} from './base/processing_utils.js';
51	
52	import {
53	    Callable,
54	} from './utils/generic.js';
55	
56	import {
57	    dispatchCallback,
58	    product,
59	} from './utils/core.js';
60	import {
61	    softmax,
62	    max,
63	    round,

77	
78	
79	/**
80	 * @typedef {string | RawImage | URL | Blob | HTMLCanvasElement | OffscreenCanvas} ImageInput
81	 * @typedef {ImageInput|ImageInput[]} ImagePipelineInputs
82	 */
83	
84	/**
85	 * Prepare images for further tasks.
86	 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} images images to prepare.
87	 * @returns {Promise<RawImage[]>} returns processed images.
88	 * @private
89	 */
90	async function prepareImages(images) {
91	    if (!Array.isArray(images)) {
92	        images = [images];
93	    }
94	
95	    // Possibly convert any non-images to images
96	    return await Promise.all(images.map(x => RawImage.read(x)));
97	}
98	
99	/**
100	 * @typedef {string | URL | Float32Array | Float64Array} AudioInput
101	 * @typedef {AudioInput|AudioInput[]} AudioPipelineInputs
102	 */
103	
104	/**
105	 * Prepare audios for further tasks.
106	 * @param {AudioPipelineInputs} audios audios to prepare.
107	 * @param {number} sampling_rate sampling rate of the audios.
108	 * @returns {Promise<Float32Array[]>} The preprocessed audio data.
109	 * @private
110	 */
111	async function prepareAudios(audios, sampling_rate) {
112	    if (!Array.isArray(audios)) {
113	        audios = [audios];
114	    }
115	
116	    return await Promise.all(audios.map(x => {
117	        if (typeof x === 'string' || x instanceof URL) {
118	            return read_audio(x, sampling_rate);
119	        } else if (x instanceof Float64Array) {
120	            return new Float32Array(x);
121	        }
122	        return x;
123	    }));
124	}
125	
126	/**
127	 * @typedef {Object} BoundingBox
128	 * @property {number} xmin The minimum x coordinate of the bounding box.
129	 * @property {number} ymin The minimum y coordinate of the bounding box.
130	 * @property {number} xmax The maximum x coordinate of the bounding box.
131	 * @property {number} ymax The maximum y coordinate of the bounding box.
132	 */
133	
134	/**
135	 * Helper function to convert list [xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax] into object { "xmin": xmin, ... }
136	 * @param {number[]} box The bounding box as a list.
137	 * @param {boolean} asInteger Whether to cast to integers.
138	 * @returns {BoundingBox} The bounding box as an object.
139	 * @private
140	 */
141	function get_bounding_box(box, asInteger) {
142	    if (asInteger) {
143	        box = box.map(x => x | 0);
144	    }
145	    const [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] = box;
146	
147	    return { xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax };
148	}
149	
150	
151	/**
152	 * @callback DisposeType Disposes the item.
153	 * @returns {Promise<void>} A promise that resolves when the item has been disposed.
154	 *
155	 * @typedef {Object} Disposable
156	 * @property {DisposeType} dispose A promise that resolves when the pipeline has been disposed.
157	 */
158	
159	/**
160	 * The Pipeline class is the class from which all pipelines inherit.
161	 * Refer to this class for methods shared across different pipelines.
162	 */
163	export class Pipeline extends Callable {
164	    /**
165	     * Create a new Pipeline.
166	     * @param {Object} options An object containing the following properties:
167	     * @param {string} [options.task] The task of the pipeline. Useful for specifying subtasks.
168	     * @param {PreTrainedModel} [options.model] The model used by the pipeline.
169	     * @param {PreTrainedTokenizer} [options.tokenizer=null] The tokenizer used by the pipeline (if any).
170	     * @param {Processor} [options.processor=null] The processor used by the pipeline (if any).
171	     */
172	    constructor({ task, model, tokenizer = null, processor = null }) {
173	        super();
174	        this.task = task;
175	        this.model = model;
176	        this.tokenizer = tokenizer;
177	        this.processor = processor;
178	    }
179	
180	    /** @type {DisposeType} */
181	    async dispose() {
182	        await this.model.dispose();
183	    }
184	}
185	
186	/**
187	 * @typedef {Object} ModelTokenizerConstructorArgs
188	 * @property {string} task The task of the pipeline. Useful for specifying subtasks.
189	 * @property {PreTrainedModel} model The model used by the pipeline.
190	 * @property {PreTrainedTokenizer} tokenizer The tokenizer used by the pipeline.
191	 *
192	 * @typedef {ModelTokenizerConstructorArgs} TextPipelineConstructorArgs An object used to instantiate a text-based pipeline.
193	 */
194	
195	/**
196	 * @typedef {Object} ModelProcessorConstructorArgs
197	 * @property {string} task The task of the pipeline. Useful for specifying subtasks.
198	 * @property {PreTrainedModel} model The model used by the pipeline.
199	 * @property {Processor} processor The processor used by the pipeline.
200	 *
201	 * @typedef {ModelProcessorConstructorArgs} AudioPipelineConstructorArgs An object used to instantiate an audio-based pipeline.
202	 * @typedef {ModelProcessorConstructorArgs} ImagePipelineConstructorArgs An object used to instantiate an image-based pipeline.
203	 */
204	
205	
206	/**
207	 * @typedef {Object} ModelTokenizerProcessorConstructorArgs
208	 * @property {string} task The task of the pipeline. Useful for specifying subtasks.
209	 * @property {PreTrainedModel} model The model used by the pipeline.
210	 * @property {PreTrainedTokenizer} tokenizer The tokenizer used by the pipeline.
211	 * @property {Processor} processor The processor used by the pipeline.
212	 *
213	 * @typedef {ModelTokenizerProcessorConstructorArgs} TextAudioPipelineConstructorArgs An object used to instantiate a text- and audio-based pipeline.
214	 * @typedef {ModelTokenizerProcessorConstructorArgs} TextImagePipelineConstructorArgs An object used to instantiate a text- and image-based pipeline.
215	 */
216	
217	/**
218	 * @typedef {Object} TextClassificationSingle
219	 * @property {string} label The label predicted.
220	 * @property {number} score The corresponding probability.
221	 * @typedef {TextClassificationSingle[]} TextClassificationOutput
222	 *
223	 * @typedef {Object} TextClassificationPipelineOptions Parameters specific to text classification pipelines.
224	 * @property {number} [top_k=1] The number of top predictions to be returned.
225	 *
226	 * @callback TextClassificationPipelineCallback Classify the text(s) given as inputs.
227	 * @param {string|string[]} texts The input text(s) to be classified.
228	 * @param {TextClassificationPipelineOptions} [options] The options to use for text classification.
229	 * @returns {Promise<TextClassificationOutput|TextClassificationOutput[]>} An array or object containing the predicted labels and scores.
230	 *
231	 * @typedef {TextPipelineConstructorArgs & TextClassificationPipelineCallback & Disposable} TextClassificationPipelineType
232	 */
233	
234	/**
235	 * Text classification pipeline using any `ModelForSequenceClassification`.
236	 *
237	 * **Example:** Sentiment-analysis w/ `Xenova/distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english`.
238	 * ```javascript
239	 * const classifier = await pipeline('sentiment-analysis', 'Xenova/distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english');
240	 * const output = await classifier('I love transformers!');
241	 * // [{ label: 'POSITIVE', score: 0.999788761138916 }]
242	 * ```
243	 *
244	 * **Example:** Multilingual sentiment-analysis w/ `Xenova/bert-base-multilingual-uncased-sentiment` (and return top 5 classes).
245	 * ```javascript
246	 * const classifier = await pipeline('sentiment-analysis', 'Xenova/bert-base-multilingual-uncased-sentiment');
247	 * const output = await classifier('Le meilleur film de tous les temps.', { top_k: 5 });
248	 * // [

253	 * //   { label: '2 stars', score: 0.0009423971059732139 }
254	 * // ]
255	 * ```
256	 *
257	 * **Example:** Toxic comment classification w/ `Xenova/toxic-bert` (and return all classes).
258	 * ```javascript
259	 * const classifier = await pipeline('text-classification', 'Xenova/toxic-bert');
260	 * const output = await classifier('I hate you!', { top_k: null });
261	 * // [
262	 * //   { label: 'toxic', score: 0.9593140482902527 },

3017	/**

3019	 * @property {Tensor} predicted_depth The raw depth map predicted by the model.
3020	 * @property {RawImage} depth The processed depth map as an image (with the same size as the input image).
3021	 *
3022	 * @callback DepthEstimationPipelineCallback Predicts the depth for the image(s) passed as inputs.
3023	 * @param {ImagePipelineInputs} images The images to compute depth for.
3024	 * @returns {Promise<DepthEstimationPipelineOutput|DepthEstimationPipelineOutput[]>} An image or a list of images containing result(s).
3025	 *
3026	 * @typedef {ImagePipelineConstructorArgs & DepthEstimationPipelineCallback & Disposable} DepthEstimationPipelineType
3027	 */

3029	/**
3030	 * Depth estimation pipeline using any `AutoModelForDepthEstimation`. This pipeline predicts the depth of an image.
3031	 *
3032	 * **Example:** Depth estimation w/ `Xenova/dpt-hybrid-midas`
3033	 * ```javascript
3034	 * const depth_estimator = await pipeline('depth-estimation', 'Xenova/dpt-hybrid-midas');
3035	 * const url = 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xenova/transformers.js-docs/resolve/main/cats.jpg';
3036	 * const out = await depth_estimator(url);
3037	 * // {
3038	 * //   predicted_depth: Tensor {
3039	 * //     dims: [ 384, 384 ],
3040	 * //     type: 'float32',
3041	 * //     data: Float32Array(147456) [ 542.859130859375, 545.2833862304688, 546.1649169921875, ... ],
3042	 * //     size: 147456
3043	 * //   },
3044	 * //   depth: RawImage {
3045	 * //     data: Uint8Array(307200) [ 86, 86, 86, ... ],
3046	 * //     width: 640,
3047	 * //     height: 480,
3048	 * //     channels: 1
3049	 * //   }
3050	 * // }
3051	 * ```
3052	 */
3053	export class DepthEstimationPipeline extends (/** @type {new (options: ImagePipelineConstructorArgs) => DepthEstimationPipelineType} */ (Pipeline)) {
3054	    /**
3055	     * Create a new DepthEstimationPipeline.
3056	     * @param {ImagePipelineConstructorArgs} options An object used to instantiate the pipeline.
3057	     */
3058	    constructor(options) {
3059	        super(options);
3060	    }
3061	
3062	    /** @type {DepthEstimationPipelineCallback} */
3063	    async _call(images) {
3064	
3065	        const preparedImages = await prepareImages(images);
3066	
3067	        const inputs = await this.processor(preparedImages);
3068	        const { predicted_depth } = await this.model(inputs);
3069	
3070	        const toReturn = [];
3071	        for (let i = 0; i < preparedImages.length; ++i) {
3072	            const batch = predicted_depth[i];
3073	            const [height, width] = batch.dims.slice(-2);
3074	            const [new_width, new_height] = preparedImages[i].size;
3075	
3076	            // Interpolate to original size
3077	            const prediction = (await interpolate_4d(batch.view(1, 1, height, width), {
3078	                size: [new_height, new_width],
3079	                mode: 'bilinear',
3080	            })).view(new_height, new_width);
3081	
3082	            const minval = /** @type {number} */(prediction.min().item());
3083	            const maxval = /** @type {number} */(prediction.max().item());
3084	            const formatted = prediction.sub(minval).div_(maxval - minval).mul_(255).to('uint8').unsqueeze(0);
3085	            const depth = RawImage.fromTensor(formatted);
3086	            toReturn.push({
3087	                predicted_depth: prediction,
3088	                depth,
3089	            });
3090	        }
3091	
3092	        return toReturn.length > 1 ? toReturn : toReturn[0];
3093	    }
3094	}

3516	/**
3517	 * Helper function to get applicable model, tokenizer, or processor classes for a given model.
3518	 * @param {Map<string, any>} mapping The mapping of names to classes, arrays of classes, or null.
3519	 * @param {string} model The name of the model to load.
3520	 * @param {import('./utils/hub.js').PretrainedOptions} pretrainedOptions The options to pass to the `from_pretrained` method.
3521	 * @private
3522	 */
3523	async function loadItems(mapping, model, pretrainedOptions) {
3524	
3525	    const result = Object.create(null);
3526	
3527	    /**@type {Promise[]} */
3528	    const promises = [];
3529	    for (const [name, cls] of mapping.entries()) {
3530	        if (!cls) continue;
3531	
3532	        /**@type {Promise} */
3533	        let promise;
3534	        if (Array.isArray(cls)) {
3535	            promise = new Promise(async (resolve, reject) => {
3536	                let e;
3537	                for (const c of cls) {
3538	                    if (c === null) {
3539	                        // If null, we resolve it immediately, meaning the relevant
3540	                        // class was not found, but it is optional.
3541	                        resolve(null);
3542	                        return;
3543	                    }
3544	                    try {
3545	                        resolve(await c.from_pretrained(model, pretrainedOptions));
3546	                        return;
3547	                    } catch (err) {
3548	                        if (err.message?.includes('Unsupported model type')) {
3549	                            // If the error is due to an unsupported model type, we
3550	                            // save the error and try the next class.
3551	                            e = err;
3552	                        } else if (err.message?.includes('Could not locate file')) {
3553	                            e = err;
3554	                        } else {
3555	                            reject(err);
3556	                            return;
3557	                        }
3558	
3559	                    }
3560	                }
3561	                reject(e);
3562	            })
3563	        } else {
3564	            promise = cls.from_pretrained(model, pretrainedOptions);
3565	        }
3566	
3567	        result[name] = promise;
3568	        promises.push(promise);
3569	    }
3570	
3571	    // Wait for all promises to resolve (in parallel)
3572	    await Promise.all(promises);
3573

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create the final report with all the findings:

## Comprehensive Report: Transformers.js Multimodal Pipeline Architecture

Based on my thorough exploration of the Transformers.js codebase, here's a complete analysis of how tokenizers and processors work together for multimodal pipelines:

---

### 1. **EXISTING IMAGE+TEXT MULTIMODAL PIPELINES**

#### A. ImageToTextPipeline
**Location:** `/src/pipelines.js` (lines 1975-2005)

This pipeline processes images and generates text descriptions. Key implementation:
- Takes images as input, optionally with `generate_kwargs`
- Uses `this.processor()` to convert images → `pixel_values` tensor
- Calls `this.model.generate()` with pixel values
- Returns decoded text via `this.tokenizer.batch_decode()`
- Supports both single and batch processing

**_call() signature:**
```javascript
async _call(images, generate_kwargs = {})
```

#### B. DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline
**Location:** `/src/pipelines.js` (lines 2732-2779)

Combines image understanding with question answering:
- Takes both `image` and `question` parameters
- Preprocesses image via `this.processor()` → `pixel_values`
- Tokenizes question with special task template: `<s_docvqa><s_question>${question}</s_question><s_answer>`
- Uses `this.model.generate()` with `decoder_input_ids` from task prompt
- Parses answer from output using regex: `/<s_answer>(.*?)<\/s_answer>/`
- Returns structured format: `[{ answer }]`

**_call() signature:**
```javascript
async _call(image, question, generate_kwargs = {})
```

#### C. Vision-Language Models (LLaVA, Qwen2-VL, etc.)

No dedicated VQA pipeline exists in current code, but the models themselves support this through:
- **LLaVA** (LlavaForConditionalGeneration): Vision encoder + language model
- **Qwen2-VL** (Qwen2VLForConditionalGeneration): Handles multi-image + multi-video with special position encoding
- **Florence-2** (Florence2ForConditionalGeneration): Encoder-decoder architecture with image-text merging

---

### 2. **CHAT TEMPLATES & APPLY_CHAT_TEMPLATE METHOD**

**Location:** `/src/tokenizers.js` (lines 3296-3354)

#### Overview
The `apply_chat_template()` method converts conversation arrays into model-ready input_ids using Jinja2-style templates stored in tokenizer configs.

#### Method Signature:
```javascript
apply_chat_template(conversation, {
    tools = null,              // For tool-use models (Claude, etc.)
    documents = null,          // For RAG models
    chat_template = null,      // Override template
    add_generation_prompt = false,  // Adds generation prompt token
    tokenize = true,           // Whether to tokenize output
    padding = false,           // Padding mode
    truncation = false,        // Truncation mode
    max_length = null,         // Max sequence length
    return_tensor = true,      // Return as tensor or list
    return_dict = false,       // Return dict instead of input_ids
    tokenizer_kwargs = {},     // Pass to underlying tokenizer
    ...kwargs                  // Additional Jinja2 variables
} = {})
```

#### Internal Flow:
1. **Get template:** Calls `this.get_chat_template()` to resolve template (handles multiple templates for tool-use models)
2. **Compile template:** Uses Template class (Jinja2 engine) with caching for performance
3. **Render:** Passes conversation array + special tokens + kwargs to template engine
4. **Tokenize (if enabled):** Runs rendered text through tokenizer with `add_special_tokens: false`
5. **Return:** Either input_ids tensor or plain rendered text

#### Template Resolution Priority:
1. User-provided `chat_template` parameter
2. Named template from tokenizer config (e.g., 'tool_use' for tools)
3. Default template from tokenizer config
4. Throws error if no template found

#### Key Methods:
- **get_chat_template()**: Returns appropriate template string with fallback logic
- Uses `_compiled_template_cache` (Map) to avoid recompilation
- Special tokens automatically injected: `eos_token`, `bos_token`, `unk_token`, etc.

#### Example Usage (from test):
```javascript
const conversation = [
    {
        role: "user",
        content: [{ type: "image" }, { type: "text", text: "Describe this image." }],
    },
];
const text = processor.apply_chat_template(conversation, {
    add_generation_prompt: true,
});
```

---

### 3. **GENERATE() METHOD PARAMETERS & FLOW**

**Location:** `/src/models.js` (lines 1735-1944)

#### Method Signature:
```javascript
async generate({
    inputs = null,
    generation_config = null,
    logits_processor = null,
    stopping_criteria = null,
    streamer = null,
    ...kwargs  // Additional generation parameters
})
```

#### Key Flow Steps:

1. **Validate model class** → Ensures model supports `.generate()`
2. **Prepare generation config** → Merges defaults, model config, user config, and kwargs
3. **Prepare model inputs** → Handles encoder-decoder vs decoder-only architectures
4. **Prepare encoder outputs** (if encoder-decoder)
5. **Prepare decoder input_ids** (from generation_config or kwargs)
6. **Setup stopping criteria** → MaxLengthCriteria, EosTokenCriteria, custom criteria
7. **Initialize sampler** → LogitsSampler for greedy/sampling/beam search
8. **Autoregressive loop** → Until stopping criteria met:
   - Run model forward pass
   - Apply logits processors
   - Sample next token
   - Update model_inputs with past_key_values for caching
9. **Return sequences** or full GenerationOutput (if return_dict_in_generate=True)

#### Input Handling:
- **is_encoder_decoder=true**: Encoder outputs created from inputs, then decoder generates
- **is_encoder_decoder=false**: Input_ids directly fed to decoder-only model

#### Special Handling for Multimodal:
- Models like LLaVA/Qwen2-VL have `_prepare_encoder_decoder_kwargs_for_generation()` override
- Encodes images separately via vision encoder sessions
- Merges image features with text embeddings at token positions marked by `image_token_id`

---

### 4. **GENERATION CONFIG HANDLING**

**Location:** `/src/generation/configuration_utils.js` (lines 11-387)

#### GenerationConfig Class
Centralized config object with all generation parameters:

**Length Control:**
- `max_length` (default: 20) - Total output length including input
- `max_new_tokens` - Only new tokens generated (preferred over max_length)
- `min_length`, `min_new_tokens`

**Strategy Parameters:**
- `do_sample` (bool, default: false) - Use sampling vs greedy
- `num_beams` (default: 1) - Beam search width
- `num_beam_groups` - Diverse beam search
- `penalty_alpha` - Contrastive search value

**Sampling Parameters:**
- `temperature` (default: 1.0) - Logit scaling
- `top_k` (default: 50) - Keep top-k tokens
- `top_p` (default: 1.0) - Nucleus sampling threshold
- `typical_p` (default: 1.0) - Locally typical sampling
- `epsilon_cutoff`, `eta_cutoff` - Truncation sampling variants

**Output Control:**
- `repetition_penalty` (default: 1.0) - Penalize repeated sequences
- `no_repeat_ngram_size` (default: 0) - Block n-gram repetition
- `bad_words_ids` - Forbidden token sequences
- `force_words_ids` - Must-include tokens
- `forced_bos_token_id`, `forced_eos_token_id` - Force specific tokens

**Encoder-Decoder Specific:**
- `encoder_no_repeat_ngram_size` - Repeat n-grams not in encoder input
- `decoder_start_token_id` - Initial decoder token

**Output Control:**
- `output_attentions` (bool) - Return attention weights
- `output_hidden_states` (bool) - Return hidden states
- `output_scores` (bool) - Return prediction scores
- `return_dict_in_generate` (bool) - Return ModelOutput vs tuple

#### Config Merging (_prepare_generation_config):
1. Create base config from model.config
2. Extract special keys: decoder, generator, text_config
3. Apply model's generation_config if exists
4. Override with user-provided generation_config
5. Override with kwargs (only keys that exist in GenerationConfig)

---

### 5. **AUTOMODELFORIMAGETATOTEXT CLASS**

**Location:** `/src/models.js` (line 8736)

```javascript
export class AutoModelForImageTextToText extends PretrainedMixin {
    static MODEL_CLASS_MAPPINGS = [MODEL_FOR_IMAGE_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES];
}
```

#### Purpose:
Factory class that auto-selects appropriate vision-language model based on `config.model_type`.

#### Supported Model Types:
- LLaVA
- LLaVA-OneVision
- Qwen2-VL
- Qwen2VL
- Florence-2
- Phi-3-V
- PaliGemma
- Idefics3
- SmolVLM
- Mistral-3
- Moondream-1
- And others with ForConditionalGeneration variants

#### Model Loading:
When instantiated via `AutoModelForImageTextToText.from_pretrained()`:
1. Loads multiple ONNX sessions:
   - `embed_tokens` - Text encoding
   - `vision_encoder` - Image encoding
   - `decoder_model_merged` - Language model decoder
   - Optional: `model` (if encoder-decoder)
2. Loads generation_config from model config
3. Handles special model types (Phi3V, Supertonic, etc.)

---

### 6. **HOW PROCESSORS BRIDGE IMAGES & TEXT**

#### Processor Pattern:
Processors combine ImageProcessor + Tokenizer + Feature Extractor:

```javascript
// From test example:
const { input_ids, attention_mask, pixel_values, image_grid_thw } = 
    await processor(text, images.white_image);
```

**Processor handles:**
1. **Text input** → Tokenizer (convert text to input_ids)
2. **Image input** → ImageProcessor (resize, normalize, convert to pixel_values)
3. **Special outputs** (model-specific):
   - pixel_values: Tensor of shape [channels, height, width]
   - image_grid_thw: Grid dimensions for Qwen2-VL multi-image support
   - image_attention_mask: For masking image regions (Idefics3, Florence-2)

#### For Qwen2-VL (Complex Example):
- Handles variable image sizes
- Returns `image_grid_thw`: temporal, height, width dimensions
- Model's `get_rope_index()` uses this for 3D position embeddings
- Special handling for mixed images + videos

---

### 7. **MULTIMODAL MODEL ARCHITECTURES**

#### A. LLaVA-style Models (Decoder-Only):
```
Input: [TEXT_TOKEN, IMAGE_TOKEN, TEXT_TOKEN, ...]
         ↓
Tokenizer/Processor:
- Tokenize text → input_ids
- Encode image → image_features (via vision_encoder)
         ↓
Model Forward (_merge_input_ids_with_image_features):
- Find IMAGE_TOKEN positions in input_ids
- Replace those positions' embeddings with image_features
- Pass merged embeddings through language model decoder
         ↓
Output: logits for next token generation
```

**Key merging function** (lines 636-665):
- Iterates through batch
- Finds each modality token position
- Directly overwrites embedding at that position with modality features
- Preserves attention_mask

#### B. Florence-2 Style (Encoder-Decoder):
```
Input: [IMAGE_EMBEDS, TASK_PROMPT_TEXT]
         ↓
Encoder:
- Image features concatenated with task embeddings
- Process through encoder layers
         ↓
Decoder:
- Generates output autoregressively
- Cross-attends to encoder outputs
         ↓
Output: Text generation
```

#### C. Qwen2-VL (Complex Multi-Image):
```
Special handling:
- 3D position embeddings (temporal, height, width)
- Multiple images/videos in single sequence
- image_grid_thw provides vision grid dimensions
- get_rope_index() calculates rotary position embeddings
```

---

### 8. **KEY INPUT/OUTPUT TENSORS FOR MULTIMODAL**

#### Typical Processor Output:
```javascript
{
    input_ids: Tensor [batch_size, seq_length],
    attention_mask: Tensor [batch_size, seq_length],
    pixel_values: Tensor [batch_size*num_patches, height, width, channels],
    // Model-specific:
    image_grid_thw: Tensor [num_images, 3],  // Qwen2-VL
    image_attention_mask: Tensor [...],       // Florence-2, Idefics3
    pixel_attention_mask: Tensor [...],       // Idefics3
}
```

#### Model Forward Parameters:
```javascript
{
    input_ids,           // From tokenizer
    attention_mask,      // From tokenizer
    pixel_values,        // From image processor
    position_ids,        // Auto-generated or model-specific (e.g., Qwen2-VL)
    past_key_values,     // For KV-cache during generation
    inputs_embeds,       // Alternative to input_ids (pre-embedded)
    image_grid_thw,      // Vision grid dimensions
    image_sizes,         // For Phi3-V
}
```

---

### 9. **GENERATION-SPECIFIC FLOWS FOR MULTIMODAL**

#### First Token Generation:
- Full input sequence passed (image tokens + text tokens)
- Image encoder runs once (expensive)
- Image features merged at token positions
- Full sequence attention computed
- Output logits for last position

#### Subsequent Tokens (with KV-cache):
- Only new token(s) passed
- Image features already in `past_key_values` (if cached)
- Decoder-only forward pass
- Much faster generation

#### Special Case - Qwen2-VL:
- `get_rope_index()` must be called to compute 3D position embeddings
- Called during forward pass to determine position_ids
- Accounts for mixed text + image tokens in sequence

---

### 10. **PRACTICAL USAGE EXAMPLE (from tests)**

```javascript
// Load processor (handles both tokenizer + image processor)
const processor = await AutoProcessor.from_pretrained("Xenova/qwen2-vl");

// Prepare conversation with image + text
const conversation = [
    {
        role: "user",
        content: [
            { type: "image" },
            { type: "text", text: "Describe this image." }
        ],
    },
];

// Apply chat template (converts to model input format)
const text = processor.apply_chat_template(conversation, {
    add_generation_prompt: true,
});

// Process text + image together
const { input_ids, attention_mask, pixel_values, image_grid_thw } = 
    await processor(text, image);

// Load model
const model = await AutoModelForImageTextToText.from_pretrained("Xenova/qwen2-vl");

// Generate response
const output = await model.generate({
    inputs: input_ids,
    attention_mask,
    pixel_values,
    image_grid_thw,
    max_new_tokens: 128,
    temperature: 0.7,
});

// Decode output
const text_output = processor.tokenizer.decode(output[0]);
```

---

### Summary Table

| Component | Location | Purpose |
|-----------|----------|---------|
| ImageToTextPipeline | pipelines.js:1975 | Simple image→text generation |
| DocumentQAPipeline | pipelines.js:2732 | Image+question→answer |
| apply_chat_template | tokenizers.js:3296 | Convert conversations to input_ids |
| generate() | models.js:1735 | Autoregressive text generation |
| GenerationConfig | generation/configuration_utils.js:11 | All generation parameters |
| AutoModelForImageTextToText | models.js:8736 | Factory for vision-language models |
| LlavaForConditionalGeneration | models.js:3704 | LLaVA model implementation |
| Qwen2VLForConditionalGeneration | models.js:4803 | Qwen2-VL with 3D position embeddings |
| Florence2ForConditionalGeneration | models.js:3740 | Encoder-decoder vision-language model |
| default_merge_input_ids_with_image_features | models.js:668 | Core multimodal merging logic |

- `temperature` (default: 1.0) - Logit scaling
- `top_k` (default: 50) - Keep top-k tokens
- `top_p` (default: 1.0) - Nucleus sampling threshold
- `typical_p` (default: 1.0) - Locally typical sampling
- `epsilon_cutoff`, `eta_cutoff` - Truncation sampling variants

**Output Control:**
- `repetition_penalty` (default: 1.0) - Penalize repeated sequences
- `no_repeat_ngram_size` (default: 0) - Block n-gram repetition
- `bad_words_ids` - Forbidden token sequences
- `force_words_ids` - Must-include tokens
- `forced_bos_token_id`, `forced_eos_token_id` - Force specific tokens

**Encoder-Decoder Specific:**
- `encoder_no_repeat_ngram_size` - Repeat n-grams not in encoder input
- `decoder_start_token_id` - Initial decoder token

**Output Control:**
- `output_attentions` (bool) - Return attention weights
- `output_hidden_states` (bool) - Return hidden states
- `output_scores` (bool) - Return prediction scores
- `return_dict_in_generate` (bool) - Return ModelOutput vs tuple

#### Config Merging (_prepare_generation_config):
1. Create base config from model.config
2. Extract special keys: decoder, generator, text_config
3. Apply model's generation_config if exists
4. Override with user-provided generation_config
5. Override with kwargs (only keys that exist in GenerationConfig)

---

### 5. **AUTOMODELFORIMAGETATOTEXT CLASS**

**Location:** `/src/models.js` (line 8736)

```javascript
export class AutoModelForImageTextToText extends PretrainedMixin {
    static MODEL_CLASS_MAPPINGS = [MODEL_FOR_IMAGE_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES];
}
```

#### Purpose:
Factory class that auto-selects appropriate vision-language model based on `config.model_type`.

#### Supported Model Types:
- LLaVA
- LLaVA-OneVision
- Qwen2-VL
- Qwen2VL
- Florence-2
- Phi-3-V
- PaliGemma
- Idefics3
- SmolVLM
- Mistral-3
- Moondream-1
- And others with ForConditionalGeneration variants

#### Model Loading:
When instantiated via `AutoModelForImageTextToText.from_pretrained()`:
1. Loads multiple ONNX sessions:
   - `embed_tokens` - Text encoding
   - `vision_encoder` - Image encoding
   - `decoder_model_merged` - Language model decoder
   - Optional: `model` (if encoder-decoder)
2. Loads generation_config from model config
3. Handles special model types (Phi3V, Supertonic, etc.)

---

### 6. **HOW PROCESSORS BRIDGE IMAGES & TEXT**

#### Processor Pattern:
Processors combine ImageProcessor + Tokenizer + Feature Extractor:

```javascript
// From test example:
const { input_ids, attention_mask, pixel_values, image_grid_thw } = 
    await processor(text, images.white_image);
```

**Processor handles:**
1. **Text input** → Tokenizer (convert text to input_ids)
2. **Image input** → ImageProcessor (resize, normalize, convert to pixel_values)
3. **Special outputs** (model-specific):
   - pixel_values: Tensor of shape [channels, height, width]
   - image_grid_thw: Grid dimensions for Qwen2-VL multi-image support
   - image_attention_mask: For masking image regions (Idefics3, Florence-2)

#### For Qwen2-VL (Complex Example):
- Handles variable image sizes
- Returns `image_grid_thw`: temporal, height, width dimensions
- Model's `get_rope_index()` uses this for 3D position embeddings
- Special handling for mixed images + videos

---

### 7. **MULTIMODAL MODEL ARCHITECTURES**

#### A. LLaVA-style Models (Decoder-Only):
```
Input: [TEXT_TOKEN, IMAGE_TOKEN, TEXT_TOKEN, ...]
         ↓
Tokenizer/Processor:
- Tokenize text → input_ids
- Encode image → image_features (via vision_encoder)
         ↓
Model Forward (_merge_input_ids_with_image_features):
- Find IMAGE_TOKEN positions in input_ids
- Replace those positions' embeddings with image_features
- Pass merged embeddings through language model decoder
         ↓
Output: logits for next token generation
```

**Key merging function** (lines 636-665):
- Iterates through batch
- Finds each modality token position
- Directly overwrites embedding at that position with modality features
- Preserves attention_mask

#### B. Florence-2 Style (Encoder-Decoder):
```
Input: [IMAGE_EMBEDS, TASK_PROMPT_TEXT]
         ↓
Encoder:
- Image features concatenated with task embeddings
- Process through encoder layers
         ↓
Decoder:
- Generates output autoregressively
- Cross-attends to encoder outputs
         ↓
Output: Text generation
```

#### C. Qwen2-VL (Complex Multi-Image):
```
Special handling:
- 3D position embeddings (temporal, height, width)
- Multiple images/videos in single sequence
- image_grid_thw provides vision grid dimensions
- get_rope_index() calculates rotary position embeddings
```

---

### 8. **KEY INPUT/OUTPUT TENSORS FOR MULTIMODAL**

#### Typical Processor Output:
```javascript
{
    input_ids: Tensor [batch_size, seq_length],
    attention_mask: Tensor [batch_size, seq_length],
    pixel_values: Tensor [batch_size*num_patches, height, width, channels],
    // Model-specific:
    image_grid_thw: Tensor [num_images, 3],  // Qwen2-VL
    image_attention_mask: Tensor [...],       // Florence-2, Idefics3
    pixel_attention_mask: Tensor [...],       // Idefics3
}
```

#### Model Forward Parameters:
```javascript
{
    input_ids,           // From tokenizer
    attention_mask,      // From tokenizer
    pixel_values,        // From image processor
    position_ids,        // Auto-generated or model-specific (e.g., Qwen2-VL)
    past_key_values,     // For KV-cache during generation
    inputs_embeds,       // Alternative to input_ids (pre-embedded)
    image_grid_thw,      // Vision grid dimensions
    image_sizes,         // For Phi3-V
}
```

---

### 9. **GENERATION-SPECIFIC FLOWS FOR MULTIMODAL**

#### First Token Generation:
- Full input sequence passed (image tokens + text tokens)
- Image encoder runs once (expensive)
- Image features merged at token positions
- Full sequence attention computed
- Output logits for last position

#### Subsequent Tokens (with KV-cache):
- Only new token(s) passed
- Image features already in `past_key_values` (if cached)
- Decoder-only forward pass
- Much faster generation

#### Special Case - Qwen2-VL:
- `get_rope_index()` must be called to compute 3D position embeddings
- Called during forward pass to determine position_ids
- Accounts for mixed text + image tokens in sequence

---

### 10. **PRACTICAL USAGE EXAMPLE (from tests)**

```javascript
// Load processor (handles both tokenizer + image processor)
const processor = await AutoProcessor.from_pretrained("Xenova/qwen2-vl");

// Prepare conversation with image + text
const conversation = [
    {
        role: "user",
        content: [
            { type: "image" },
            { type: "text", text: "Describe this image." }
        ],
    },
];

// Apply chat template (converts to model input format)
const text = processor.apply_chat_template(conversation, {
    add_generation_prompt: true,
});

// Process text + image together
const { input_ids, attention_mask, pixel_values, image_grid_thw } = 
    await processor(text, image);

// Load model
const model = await AutoModelForImageTextToText.from_pretrained("Xenova/qwen2-vl");

// Generate response
const output = await model.generate({
    inputs: input_ids,
    attention_mask,
    pixel_values,
    image_grid_thw,
    max_new_tokens: 128,
    temperature: 0.7,
});

// Decode output
const text_output = processor.tokenizer.decode(output[0]);
```

Everything is exactly right. Here's a summary of all 5 changes made to `src/pipelines.js`:

---

### Changes made (all in `src/pipelines.js`)

**1. Import (line 44)** — Added `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the named imports from `./models.js`, placed after `AutoModelForImageFeatureExtraction`.

**2 & 3. JSDoc typedefs + class (lines 2008–2080)** — Inserted after `ImageToTextPipeline`, before `ImageClassificationPipeline`:

- `@typedef ImageTextToTextSingle` / `ImageTextToTextOutput` — output shape
- `@callback ImageTextToTextPipelineCallback` — typed call signature accepting `(images, texts, options?)`
- `@typedef ImageTextToTextPipelineType` — intersection of constructor args + callback + disposable
- `ImageTextToTextPipeline` class with:
  - Standard `constructor(options)` pattern
  - `_call(images, texts, generate_kwargs)` that:
    - Detects batching from either `images` or `texts`
    - Handles Chat objects via `processor.apply_chat_template()` (mirrors `TextGenerationPipeline`)
    - Normalises plain strings to arrays
    - Calls `this.processor(texts, preparedImages)` for unified text+image preprocessing
    - Generates via `this.model.generate({...inputs, ...generate_kwargs})`
